title: Fiscal Geometry: Mapping Institutional Tension (ITI) in Education Access — A Node-Level Protocol for Rules and Fiscal Flows
author: Huang, Jim Yongzhi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This working paper presents a   Fiscal Geometry–based analytical protocol for examining educational access at the point of district entry. Rather than advancing normative claims about fairness or justice, the study develops a node-level mapping method that describes how rules and fiscal flows co-locate, sequence, and interact at institutional entry gates. 
 Using publicly available administrative documents only, the paper describes institutional tension as an observable structural condition arising from the configuration of eligibility rules, payment requirements, timing constraints, and information visibility. While the term  Institutional Tension Index (ITI)  was formalized in later work, the analytical logic developed here constitutes a methodological precursor, demonstrating how such tension can be mapped and compared descriptively within a two-axis fiscal geometry framework, without causal inference. 
 The primary contribution is a replicable node-level protocol, including standardized node tables and flow representations, designed for reuse in education, tax-financed services, and other rule-governed access systems. The framework is intended as a descriptive and audit-ready analytical language rather than a policy prescription.
